assertions.title: Eventos thinking_token chegam ao frontend via socket.io/Redis

steps.expected: For each ('thinking', token), a thinking_token event is published via realtime_event_bus
steps.observed: Test passed - thinking_token events correctly published for each token
Test passed - streaming method correctly invoked

steps.expected: thinking_start event emitted at beginning of processing
steps.observed: Test passed - thinking_start event correctly emitted with conversation_id

steps.expected: Event has type='thinking', conversationId, payload with event='thinking_token' and data.token
steps.observed: Test passed - schema verified: {type: 'thinking', conversationId, payload: {event: 'thinking_token', data: {token: '...'}}}

steps.expected: Redis-backed event bus publishes to channel configured in settings.REALTIME_REDIS_CHANNEL
steps.observed: Implementation confirmed - uses Redis.from_url() and publishes to 'neuralilux:realtime:events'

evidence.details: All thinking_token events are published via realtime_event_bus.publish(). The event bus uses Redis (settings.REDIS_URL) and publishes to channel 'neuralilux:realtime:events' (settings.REALTIME_REDIS_CHANNEL). Events include type='thinking', conversationId, payload.event='thinking_token', and data.token.

assertions.title: thinking_end event includes summary do reasoning

steps.expected: thinking_end event includes data.summary = first 120 chars of thinking_content
steps.observed: Test passed - summary correctly truncated to 120 characters

steps.expected: After streaming completes, thinking_end event emitted with summary from thinking_buffer[:120]
steps.observed: Code verified: summary = thinking_content[:120] if thinking_content else f'Intenção: {intent}'

steps.action: Verify super_agents also emit thinking_end with summary
steps.expected: super_agents/graph/nodes.py follows same pattern as agents/graph/nodes.py
steps.observed: Test test_super_agents_generate_response_node_calls_streaming passed - events emitted with summary

evidence.codeReference: app/agents/graph/nodes.py: _emit_thinking_event() called with event='thinking_end', data={'summary': summary}
evidence.details: The thinking_end event is emitted at the end of response generation in generate_response_node(). Summary is created from first 120 characters of accumulated thinking_buffer. If no thinking content exists, defaults to 'Intenção: {intent}'. Both regular agents and super_agents follow this pattern.

assertions.title: LM Studio indisponível — fallback gracioso sem crash

steps.expected: If LM Studio errors during streaming: thinking_end emitted with summary='Erro ao gerar resposta', no crash
steps.observed: Test passed - error handled gracefully, thinking_end emitted with error summary

steps.action: Review error handling in generate_response_node try/catch block
steps.expected: Exception caught, error logged, thinking_end emitted with error summary, graceful response returned
steps.observed: Code verified: except block emits thinking_end with data={'summary': 'Erro ao gerar resposta'} and returns fallback response

steps.action: Review error handling in agent_executor.py process_message
steps.expected: Exception caught at top level, error response sent to user, no unhandled exceptions
steps.observed: Code verified: try/catch in process_message() sends generic error message on failure

evidence.details: Error handling is comprehensive: (1) Streaming errors in generate_response_node emit thinking_end with 'Erro ao gerar resposta' summary, (2) Top-level errors in agent_executor.process_message() are caught and result in generic error message sent to user, (3) User receives 'Desculpe, estou com dificuldades técnicas no momento. Por favor, tente novamente em instantes. 😊' - no crashes or unhandled exceptions.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: All 3 passed (VAL-BE-008 through VAL-BE-010). Pytest execution completed successfully. All thinking events (thinking_start, thinking_token, thinking_end) are correctly published via the Redis-backed realtime_event_bus. The thinking_end event includes a summary truncated to 120 characters. Error handling is graceful with no crashes when LM Studio is unavailable.
